purpose: The GUI swarm dispatch engine — the in-app replacement for a terminal-based master agent. Reads dashboards/{id}/initialization.json, tracks dispatched/completed/failed tasks in memory, and dispatches every task whose `depends_on` is satisfied by spawning a Claude or Codex worker. Drives the full lifecycle: startSwarm, pauseSwarm, resumeSwarm, cancelSwarm, retryTask, handleProgressUpdate (called by the watcher on progress-file writes). Implements two circuit breakers: 3+ failures in same wave OR a single failure that blocks 3+ remaining tasks (or >50% of remaining tasks). When the breaker trips, spawns a Claude CLI replanner with the buildReplanPrompt output, parses the returned JSON {modified, added, removed, retry}, mutates initialization.json on disk, and resumes dispatch. On swarm completion (success or partial failure), runs extractSwarmKnowledge() which harvests worker annotations from progress files, generates swarm insights (dependency_insights from CRITICAL deviations, failure_patterns from failed tasks, architecture_notes from MODERATE deviations), and merges everything into the project's `.synapse/knowledge/` PKI manifest + annotations.
gotchas: activeSwarms is in-memory only — restarting Electron loses all dispatch state. Recovery flow relies on reading progress files via handleProgressUpdate when the watcher fires for existing files at server start.

Two breakers exist and either trips: (1) 3+ failures in same wave, (2) a single failure that blocks >=3 OR >50% of remaining tasks. The blast-radius breaker is more aggressive than the documented 'circuit breaker' rule and can pause swarms even with a single failure.
When the replanner CLI fails (non-zero exit, malformed JSON, spawn error), the swarm is set to `paused`, NOT cancelled — manual user intervention required via UI.
applyReplan applies modifications in the order: removed, modified, added, retry. Retry tasks have their failed flag cleared AND their progress file deleted on disk so the worker starts fresh.
extractSwarmKnowledge runs on EVERY swarm completion — including partially failed swarms. It is wrapped in try/catch and explicitly marked non-blocking; failures only emit a warn log.

isSwarmComplete considers a task 'finished' if it is completed, failed, OR blocked by a failed dependency — meaning a long failure-chain marks the swarm complete sooner than naive task-counting suggests.

Provider switching happens at task dispatch time via `var service = swarm.provider === 'codex' ? CodexService : ClaudeCodeService;` — there is no fallback if codex is selected but its CLI is not on PATH.

primaryContexts vs additionalContexts is partitioned by path.resolve(swarm.projectPath) + path.sep prefix — symlinked project paths can land in the wrong bucket if realpath differs from the stored path.
appendLog reads + parses + rewrites the entire logs.json on every entry — for swarms that emit thousands of log lines this is O(n^2) write traffic.
The PKI insights_index in the manifest is capped at 50 entries (slice(-50)) — long-lived projects with many swarms naturally lose history beyond that window.
patterns: Single in-memory state map `activeSwarms[dashboardId] = {state, projectPath, provider, model, cliPath, dispatchedTasks, completedTasks, failedTasks, ...}`.
Reactive dispatch loop: every onTaskComplete / onTaskFailed / resumeSwarm calls dispatchReady() which scans the agents[] for tasks with all deps satisfied and spawns workers for them.
Provider-agnostic worker dispatch: ClaudeCodeService and CodexService share the spawnWorker(opts) signature, swapped at call site.
Two-phase replan: spawn CLI -> parse JSON -> mutate initialization.json on disk -> resume. The CLI runs single-turn (--print, --output-format text, no streaming).
Post-swarm knowledge extraction: deviation severity drives insight category (CRITICAL -> dependency_insights, MODERATE -> architecture_notes, failed status -> failure_patterns).
appendLog always re-reads logs.json (defensive against external writers) and pushes a fixed schema {timestamp, task_id, agent, level, message, task_name}.
conventions: All public functions return `{success, error?}` for action endpoints.
broadcastFn channels in use: `swarm-state` (state transitions only). Worker output / completion events flow through ClaudeCodeService/CodexService.
